question_text: A cyclist accelerates from rest and reaches a speed of 8 m/s after 4 seconds. The cyclist then travels at this constant speed for 6 seconds before applying the brakes and coming to a stop in 2 seconds. Sketch a speed-time graph for this journey and explain what the area under the graph represents.
correct_answer: The graph shows three sections: a diagonal line from (0,0) to (4,8) representing acceleration, a horizontal line from (4,8) to (10,8) representing constant speed, and a diagonal line from (10,8) to (12,0) representing deceleration. The area under the graph represents the total distance travelled.
explanation: A speed-time graph's area under the line always represents distance because distance = speed × time. The three sections show acceleration (triangular area), constant speed (rectangular area), and braking (triangular area), with the total area giving the journey's total distance.
